_meta.description: Curated glossary of Indian legal terms for the Document Vault 'Legal Lens'. Plain-language definitions (general legal knowledge, advocate-reviewable). NOT case law — no citations are fabricated here.
_meta.note: Definitions are concise practitioner-level explanations. Statute hints reference the governing provision where one cleanly applies (new code first, old in brackets).

terms.def: A direction that a person be released on bail in the event of arrest, sought before arrest for apprehension of a non-bailable offence.
Release of an arrested person from custody during investigation or trial, on furnishing bond and sureties.
An indefeasible right to bail when the investigating agency fails to file the charge-sheet within the statutory period (60/90 days).
An offence for which a police officer may arrest without warrant and begin investigation without a magistrate's order.
An offence for which the police cannot arrest without warrant or investigate without a magistrate's order.
An offence for which bail is a matter of right; the accused is released on furnishing bail.
A more serious offence for which bail is at the discretion of the court, not a matter of right.
The mental element of a crime — the guilty intention or knowledge that accompanies the prohibited act.
The physical element of a crime — the wrongful act or omission itself, as distinct from the mental element.
The first recorded information of a cognizable offence given to the police, which sets the criminal process in motion.
The final report filed by police after investigation, setting out the accusations and evidence against the accused.
The act of a magistrate taking judicial notice of an offence so as to initiate proceedings against the accused.
Release of an accused before charges are framed, where the court finds no sufficient ground to proceed to trial.
A judicial finding that the accused is not guilty, following trial.
A judicial finding that the accused is guilty of the offence charged.
Detention of an accused authorised by a magistrate during investigation or trial (police or judicial custody).
The document by which a client authorises an advocate to appear and act on their behalf in a case.
A written statement of facts sworn or affirmed on oath before an authorised officer, used as evidence.
A proceeding or order made in the absence of, or without hearing, the other party.
On first appearance; sufficient to establish a fact or case unless rebutted by contrary evidence.
The legal standing or right of a party to bring an action or be heard in a given matter.
A matter already finally decided by a competent court cannot be litigated again between the same parties.
Causing death by an act done with intention or knowledge likely to cause death; murder is its aggravated form.
Instigating, conspiring in, or intentionally aiding the commission of an offence.
A shared intention among several persons; each is liable for an act done in furtherance of it as if done alone.
The shared purpose of an unlawful assembly; every member is liable for offences committed in prosecution of it.
The High Court setting aside an FIR, complaint or proceeding in exercise of its inherent powers to prevent abuse of process.
A superior court's examination of the correctness, legality or propriety of a lower court's order.
A statutory remedy to have a higher court re-examine the decision of a lower court.
Examination of a witness by the opposing party, to test and challenge the testimony given.
The first examination of a witness by the party who called them.
The written statement of claim with which a plaintiff institutes a civil suit.
The defendant's formal written reply to the plaint, admitting or denying its allegations.
The formal expression of a court's adjudication that conclusively determines the rights of parties in a civil suit.
A court order restraining a party from doing, or compelling them to do, a particular act.
A legal document issued by a court directing a person to appear before it as a party or witness.
A written authority from a court directing the arrest of a person or a search.
A person who undertakes responsibility, by bond, to ensure an accused released on bail appears as required.
A written undertaking by the accused, with or without sureties, to abide by bail conditions and appear in court.
An offence the victim may lawfully settle with the accused, leading to acquittal, with or without the court's permission.
Occurring before death — e.g. injuries inflicted while the victim was alive (relevant to cause of death).
Examination of a body after death to determine the cause and manner of death.
A statement by a person about the cause of their death, admissible in evidence though the maker cannot be cross-examined.
The medical reason a person died, as opined in a post-mortem / medico-legal report.
At the outset; a matter (often a petition) dismissed in limine is rejected at the threshold without detailed hearing.
On its own motion — a court taking up a matter without a formal application by any party.
Temporary relief granted to a party pending the final decision of a case.
Variant spelling of 'cognizable' — an offence police may investigate and arrest for without warrant.
Financial support a person is legally obliged to provide to a spouse, child or parent unable to maintain themselves.
